summary: No ship: the RED record matches e9f4d59/tree f873f240, but R-4 remains contradictory and R-5 can still turn GREEN with an unusable push command. No materially simpler alternative or additional critical issue found.

findings.title: Blocker: B-1 contradicts the executor contract
findings.body: B-1 assigns `gh pr list`, PR creation, and rebuild pushing to `bump-poll.yaml`, directly contradicting the earlier requirement that `ensure-bump-pr.ts` alone performs query, decision, and every remote mutation. An engineer following this increment either fails the new call-site gates or recreates R-4's split ordering and partial-failure window.
findings.recommendation: Rewrite B-1 so the workflow only prepares the deterministic local branch and commit, then invokes `ensure-bump-pr.ts`; place `gh pr list`, `git ls-remote`, push, PR creation, and auto-merge entirely inside the executor.

findings.title: Blocker: lease witnesses accept an unusable push target
findings.body: The plan claims call flags are pinned, but W2/W3 at red.sha only grep the `git push --force-with-lease=<ref>:<oid>` prefix, while the git stub accepts every non-query argv. A GREEN implementation can therefore omit or misname `origin <branch>` and still satisfy count, lease, and action assertions; live DIRTY/orphan recovery can then fail and stall deployment, leaving R-5 insufficiently proven.
findings.recommendation: Specify and assert the complete argv, such as `git push --force-with-lease=refs/heads/<branch>:<oid> origin HEAD:refs/heads/<branch>`, and make the git stub reject every unexpected push form. Re-pin red.sha and regenerate the script-owned RED record.

next_steps: Resolve the executor ownership contradiction in B-1.
Strengthen W2/W3 to validate the complete push command, then recapture RED evidence.
